notes: 800W-rated; operate ~500W each via VESC current limits (headroom). CONFIRM hall-sensor cable (3 thin hall wires + 5V + GND beside the 3 fat phase wires) before ordering - title doesn't state it. Buy 2 identical: they mirror-mount
column9: reverse one in VESC. 80/65-6 pneumatic tire can flat - consider solid/honeycomb. Fallback: https://www.amazon.com/s?k=10+inch+48V+hub+motor+hall+sensor

notes: Rated 14-60V / 4-13S - covers a 48V (54.6V max) pack. Do NOT use a 12S-rated unit (50.4V) with a 13S pack. Takes UART (speed+steer) from the ESP32; tune in VESC Tool. Wire the hall sensors for sensored mode
Includes 54.6V charger. RISK: 30A BMS may trip on hard dual-motor hill starts (peaks can hit 40-60A). Prefer a pack rated >=40A continuous discharge if you can find one
Handles the 54.6V peak. Feeds the ESP32 5V pin
Bluetooth Classic for Wiimote + UART to VESC (both 3.3V logic - no level shifter)
Use this to make/break the 48V main line - an automotive toggle is NOT rated to break ~54V DC under load and will arc/weld
On battery positive. ANL/MIDI (not ATC blade) because dual-motor peaks exceed the ~40A ATC ceiling
Wire NC contact to cut the buck/ESP32 power: ESP32 drops -> VESC loses UART -> configured failsafe brings motors to zero. See docs/electrical.md
Fine to ~60A; consider XT90 on the main battery lead for peak headroom
Nunchuk stick gives analog throttle. Genuine Nintendo pairs more reliably than clones
Swivel (not rigid) so it can pivot; rated well above full loaded weight
Mount hub-motor axles + casters + seat; size to can inner diameter
Fabricate or buy dropout brackets so both hub axles are coaxial and aligned; thread-lock
Keep center of gravity low; sit
Cover spokes; cut an eye-level visibility band
